name: Dave Holland
abstract: David Holland (born 1 October 1946) is an English double bassist, bass guitarist, cellist, composer and bandleader who has been performing and recording for five decades. He has lived in the United States since the early 1970s.

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: We made this record with Pat Metheny and Roy Haynes and Dave Holland called Like Minds... Dave had worked a lot with Chick early on, and seemed like a likely choice—and there we were.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Eventually I wound up producing an album of him with Dave Holland. It was called For Evans Sake... I convinced him, 'Come on man! Stick it out!'... Dave goes and plays with Miles.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: When I was in Miles’ band, Dave Holland and I had a growing affinity for playing together... We actually rehearsed at our 19th Street loft... and that’s how that all began.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I had never met Dave or Daniel before the studio... Dave had a tune. That’s basically it. We just played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: Warren Bernhardt and Dave Holland. We did some trio stuff up there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewee mentions 'Holland...what’s his name...' and Interviewer supplies 'Dave Holland?', but Interviewee immediately corrects himself to 'Charlie Haden' and discusses Haden's work, showing no personal connection or specific knowledge of Dave Holland beyond the name.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
